role_source.admin: Weixin user currently bound to the OpenClaw primary account slot
role_source.user: Allowed Weixin user that is not the primary account user
role_source.blocked: Sender outside allowed_users and not accepted by account binding resolver

user.description: Normal authenticated Weixin user. The ask action is whitelist-only: non-sensitive questions, processing data supplied by the user in the current request, and explicitly requested external public resources. It does not grant local-machine reads, writes, deletes, diagnostics, secrets, or local-data transfer.

admin.description: Primary-account administrator and superuser. Admin owns all currently defined permissions and also temporarily owns unspecified future permissions. Unknown admin actions are allowed with audit so the permission table can be expanded later.

action_rules.ask: Whitelist-only. May submit non-sensitive questions, process data supplied by the user in the current request, or fetch explicitly requested external public resources within resource policy. It never includes permission to read, summarize, copy, export, transmit, disclose, modify, delete, move, overwrite, clean, install, stop, restart, repair, or otherwise change local-machine data or state unless separate explicit actions are present in the active permission profile. L3 still requires confirmation.
action_rules.supplement: May add same-thread supplement messages to an active owner when supplement routing rules match.
action_rules.thread_switch: May select this user's Codex thread route.
action_rules.status_self: May see this user's own bridge status.
action_rules.repair_system: May start total computer maintenance safe-apply boundary.
action_rules.repair_bridge: May run bridge-scoped safe repair modes.
action_rules.stop: May pause bridge delivery, switch shadow mode, stop worker, and disable worker task.
action_rules.resume: May clear stop markers and restart worker.
action_rules.hardstop: May run the strongest bridge stop path.
action_rules.confirm_l3: May confirm this user's latest waiting L3 task with the configured secret.
action_rules.dashboard_send: May enqueue a dashboard-proxy message for the target user.
action_rules.dashboard_send_to_weixin: May directly send an outbound Weixin message from the dashboard.
action_rules.dashboard_cancel: May cancel a dashboard-visible active task.
action_rules.set_secret_hash: May update the high-risk confirmation secret hash.
action_rules.capability_token_admin: Admin-only. May grant, revoke, inspect, and validate temporary capability tokens. Tokens must expire and require admin regrant for renewal.
action_rules.generated_file_create: Temporary-token-only for normal users. May create new generated artifacts only inside the account-scoped bridge attachment directory, never arbitrary local paths.
action_rules.generated_file_send: Temporary-token-only for normal users. May send generated artifacts only from the account-scoped bridge attachment directory, never existing local files or sensitive data.
action_rules.reply_with_generated_artifact: Temporary-token-only for normal users. May receive a generated artifact reply produced from user-provided or non-sensitive content.
action_rules.attachment_process_user_supplied: Temporary-token-only for normal users. May process attachments supplied by the same user through the bridge resource layer.
action_rules.public_web_fetch: Temporary-token-only for normal users. May fetch explicit public web resources within the resource policy; no login/session/private resources.
action_rules.local_data_read: May read explicitly permitted local-machine data classes. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.workspace_file_read: May read workspace files. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.bridge_db_read: May read bridge database rows beyond the user's own status. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.system_diagnostics_read: May read local system diagnostics, process state, logs, and performance data. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.resource_library_read: May read the desktop Codex resource library. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.local_data_export: May send local-machine data back to Weixin or another external destination. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.local_data_write: May modify, create, move, rename, overwrite, clean, or delete explicitly permitted local-machine data classes. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.system_side_effect: May install, uninstall, start, stop, restart, repair, or otherwise change local system state. Not granted to normal user by default.
action_rules.secret_read: Normal users cannot read secrets through mobile bridge prompts. Admin requests involving secrets are superuser actions and must be audited and risk-controlled.

global_rules: Deny unknown actions by default for normal users and blocked senders.
Allow unknown actions for admin with audit because admin is the bridge superuser and owns currently unspecified future permissions.
Deny any normal-user request whose required action is not explicitly present in the active permission profile, even if the user asks directly, insists, or provides persuasive context.

For normal users, do not treat ask as a wildcard. ask is a whitelist action only for non-sensitive questions, user-provided data processing, and explicitly requested external public resources. ask does not imply local data read, local data export, file access, database access, log access, system diagnostics, resource-library access, secret access, write/delete/modify access, repair, or external side effects.

For admin, ask may cover local-machine, sensitive, or unspecified actions under superuser semantics, but Codex must identify risk, respect confirmation and audit requirements, and avoid treating user role rules as admin limits.
Normal-user requests to modify, delete, move, overwrite, clean, install, uninstall, stop, restart, repair, or otherwise change local data or local system state require explicit non-ask actions.
When a normal-user request exceeds the active permission profile, Codex must refuse that part and may provide a safe explanation or ask the primary admin to authorize a broader profile.
The permission profile in a Codex prompt is advisory for reasoning; execution entry points must enforce the same table.
A user-visible permission field should reference this table instead of embedding a long capability list.
High-risk L3 actions require the configured confirmation flow even when the profile includes the requested action or the actor is admin.

Local secrets, tokens, cookies, private keys, confirmation secrets, auth context tokens, and credential-bearing config require admin superuser authority plus audit/risk controls before any mobile bridge response may include them.

Temporary capability tokens can only be granted or renewed by the primary admin. They do not grant local data read/export/write, secret access, repairs, installs, process control, or other destructive/system side effects.

Generated artifact tokens are path-scoped to the bridge attachment area under attachments/generated/<account_id>/ so ordinary users cannot turn them into arbitrary filesystem write or send permissions.
Expired, revoked, or used-up temporary capability tokens must fail closed and require a new admin grant; the system must not auto-renew them.
